response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine Staff-level pedigree, strong TypeScript/Node.js distributed-systems experience (notably at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second), and directly relevant current work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq — exactly the AI-native engineering environment the role targets. However, Python is listed as a hard must-have and is entirely absent from the resume, skills list, and project history, which is a meaningful gap that cannot be assumed away. Distributed systems depth is real but the highest-throughput work is six-plus years old. The profile warrants a screening call to assess Python proficiency and operational readiness before committing to a full loop.
strengths: Current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq directly matches the AI-native engineering environment the role requires
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency microservices) shows the required architectural depth
Consistent Staff-level titling across multiple orgs with evidence of technical strategy, mentoring, and cross-functional tooling ownership
concerns: Python is a stated must-have and is completely absent from the resume, skills list, and every project description — no signal whatsoever
High-throughput distributed systems work is concentrated in 2018–2020 (Sociomantic); recent roles are more product-feature and tooling-focused, raising questions about current depth at scale
No explicit mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or operational ownership — a concrete requirement of this role that the resume does not address
